lessons.title: Learn: Package Management Basics (APT)
lessons.content_md:
# Lab 05: Package Management Basics

Linux package managers let you install, update, verify, and remove software safely. In Debian/Ubuntu systems, this is done with `apt`.

## Learning Objectives

- Understand repositories, package indexes, and package metadata
- Use `apt` and `apt-cache` for search and inspection
- Install and remove packages safely
- Verify installed versions and package locations

## Core Concepts

### 1) Key APT Commands

```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install <package>
sudo apt remove <package>
apt list --installed
```

### 2) Search and Inspect

```bash
apt search curl
apt-cache policy curl
apt show curl
```

### 3) Why Update First?

`apt update` refreshes package indexes from repositories so you install the latest available package metadata.

### 4) Safe Usage Rules

- Run `apt update` before install operations
- Prefer official repository packages for security
- Read package descriptions before install
- Avoid random third-party repos in beginner labs

After this Learn section, complete command practice, then exercises, then MCQ.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 1 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 1

Use read-only and metadata commands first.

```bash
apt --version
apt list --installed | head -n 10
apt search curl
apt-cache policy curl
apt show curl | head -n 15
which apt
dpkg -l | head -n 10
apt list --upgradable
apt-cache show bash | head -n 15
apt search net-tools
```

tasks.description: C01: Show apt version.
C02: List installed packages and show first lines.
C03: Search for package curl.
C04: Check apt policy for curl.
C05: Show details for curl package.
C06: Show path of apt command.
C07: List packages with dpkg output preview.
C08: List upgradable packages.
C09: Show metadata for bash package.
C10: Search for net-tools package.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 2 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 2

Practice install-state checks and package file inspection.

```bash
dpkg -s bash | head -n 10
dpkg -L bash | head -n 10
apt-cache search editor | head -n 10
apt-cache policy bash
apt show coreutils | head -n 15
dpkg -s coreutils | head -n 10
apt-cache depends curl
apt-cache rdepends curl | head -n 10
apt list --installed | grep bash
apt list --installed | grep coreutils
```

tasks.description: C11: Show status info for bash package.
C12: List first files installed by bash package.
C13: Search packages related to editor.
C14: Show apt policy for bash package.
C15: Show package details for coreutils.
C16: Show status of coreutils package.
C17: Show dependencies of curl package.
C18: Show reverse dependencies of curl.
C19: Confirm bash exists in installed package list.
C20: Confirm coreutils exists in installed package list.

lessons.title: Exercises (5)
lessons.content_md:
## Practical Exercises

Complete all package-management exercises with read/inspect operations.

- E1-E2: easy
- E3-E4: medium
- E5: medium-hard

tasks.description: E1: Find details for package wget and show first lines.
E2: Check package policy for wget.
E3: Search packages for ftp keyword and show first results.
E4: Display dependencies for wget.
E5: Verify wget in installed packages list with grep.

lessons.title: MCQ Quiz (5)
lessons.content_md:
## MCQ: Package Management Basics

Answer using option index (0-3). Example: submit 2.

tasks.description: Q1: Which command refreshes package indexes?
Q2: Which command searches packages by keyword?
Q3: Which command shows package install status details?
Q4: Which command lists files installed by a package?
Q5: What is the safest first step before installing new packages?
tasks.validation_rule: ["apt purge old packages","apt update","reboot system","delete apt cache manually"]
